title: HiLSVA: Design and Evaluation of a Human-in-the-Loop Agentic System for Scientific Visualization
abstract: arXiv:2606.26614v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents enable natural language interaction for scientific visualization (SciVis). Still, prior systems have essentially prioritized autonomy over human analytical control, thereby limiting transparency and human oversight. We present HiLSVA, a human-in-the-loop agentic system that supports mixed-initiative SciVis workflows. HiLSVA integrates a plan-first multi-agent architecture with explicit human oversight, stepwise provenance tracking, and learn-at-test-time adaptation from user feedback. The system supports fluid handoff between humans and agents through both natural language and direct manipulation of visualizations, while sandboxed execution ensures safe, reproducible workflows. In doing so, HiLSVA reframes agentic SciVis as a collaborative process that augments, rather than replaces, human analytical reasoning. We evaluate HiLSVA through representative case studies and a controlled user study with twel

Title: HiLSVA: Design and Evaluation of a Human-in-the-Loop Agentic System for Scientific Visualization
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26614v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents enable natural language interaction for scientific visualization (SciVis). Still, prior systems have essentially prioritized autonomy over human analytical control, thereby limiting transparency and human oversight. We present HiLSVA, a human-in-the-loop agentic system that supports mixed-initiative SciVis workflows. HiLSVA integrates a plan-first multi-agent architecture with explicit human oversight, stepwise provenance tracking, and learn-at-test-time adaptation from user feedback. The system supports fluid handoff between humans and agents through both natural language and direct manipulation of visualizations, while sandboxed execution ensures safe, reproducible workflows. In doing so, HiLSVA reframes agentic SciVis as a collaborative process that augments, rather than replaces, human analytical reasoning. We evaluate HiLSVA through representative case studies and a controlled user study with twel
translated_title: HiLSVA：面向科学可视化的人机协同智能体系统的设计与评估
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26614v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）智能体使得科学可视化（SciVis）能够通过自然语言进行交互。然而，先前的系统基本上优先考虑自主性而非人类的分析控制，从而限制了透明度和人类监督。我们提出了HiLSVA，这是一个支持混合主动式科学可视化工作流的人机协同智能体系统。HiLSVA集成了计划优先的多智能体架构，具有明确的人类监督、逐步来源追踪以及从用户反馈中进行的“测试时学习”自适应能力。该系统通过自然语言和对可视化内容的直接操作，支持人类与智能体之间的流畅交接，同时沙盒执行确保了安全、可重现的工作流程。通过这种方式，HiLSVA将智能体驱动的科学可视化重新定义为一种协作过程，该过程增强而非取代人类的分析推理能力。我们通过代表性案例研究和一项包含十二名参与者的受控用户研究对HiLSVA进行了评估。
tldr: 本文提出了HiLSVA系统，通过将人类监督、逐步溯源和用户反馈自适应融入计划优先的多智能体架构，将科学可视化从全自主智能体转变为增强人类分析推理的协作过程。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes HiLSVA, a human-in-the-loop agentic system that reframes scientific visualization from fully autonomous agents to a collaborative process augmenting human analytical reasoning by integrating human oversight, stepwise provenance tracking, and test-time adaptation into a plan-first multi-agent architecture.
